questions.q: What did the 1803 Louisiana Purchase do?
options: Roughly doubled the size of the United States
questions.explanation: The U.S. bought the vast Louisiana Territory from France.

options: The belief that the U.S. was destined to expand across the continent
questions.explanation: Many Americans believed the nation should stretch from the Atlantic to the Pacific.

questions.q: The Trail of Tears refers to what?
options: The forced removal of the Cherokee and other Native nations
questions.explanation: Thousands died during the forced march off their homelands.

questions.q: What was the focus of the 1848 Seneca Falls Convention?
questions.explanation: Reformers there demanded equal rights for women.

questions.q: Who was a leading voice of the abolition movement?
questions.explanation: Douglass, who had escaped slavery, was a leading abolitionist.
